steps.title: Spot the language of routine
steps.content: The passage begins with “Every few days” and describes an activity repeated “in the afternoons” with a consistent ritual. This language of recurrence signals that Grande is capturing a repeated scene from her childhood, not a singular moment.

steps.title: Match the pattern to the choices
steps.content:
The passage uses language of recurrence (“every few days,” “in the afternoons, she would”) to capture a repeated, regular scene from Grande's childhood — which is precisely what choice B describes.

The other three options focus on the wrong elements:
  • A identifies a sibling interaction (when the passage centers on Abuela and Élida)
  • C claims a relationship develops (when the passage shows a static scene)
  • D emphasizes feelings about place (when the passage emphasizes the routine itself)
.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
